impact: UsernameExistsException intentionally reveals that an account exists for that email. This matches T-096 acceptance criteria but is a deliberate auth-enumeration tradeoff worth keeping documented as product/security posture.
resolution: Documented the T-096 UsernameExistsException posture in the Cognito Patient Auth Setup runbook: sign-up intentionally uses narrow patient-facing copy that an account already exists for the submitted email, while still forbidding raw Cognito responses, provider internals, tokens, challenge/session values, stack traces, and broad disclosure in other auth flows.
title: Document UsernameExistsException account-existence disclosure posture
